title: SafeSubprocessRunner y allowlist de ejecución controlada
wave: Ola 3 — Seguridad operacional, aprobación humana y ejecución controlada

architectural_decision.reason: FUNC-SPRINT-31 extends the existing local-first policy/guard architecture with an internal controlled-execution boundary. It adds no external dependencies, no network/API usage, no new storage backend, no UI and no destructive operations. It is a constrained implementation step required before tests.run.

pass_criteria: SafeSubprocessRunner uses argument lists and never shell=True.
Commands outside the local allowlist are blocked.
cwd outside workspace is blocked by PathGuard.
Execution result is serializable as CommandResult.
block_criteria: Secrets are emitted raw in stdout/stderr.
The sprint exposes arbitrary command execution or implements tests.run prematurely.
risks: The allowlist must remain narrow until Sprint 32 integrates tests.run with policy and approval.
Output redaction is a first conservative layer and does not replace industrial secret scanning.
No full filesystem sandbox or rollback exists yet.
Allowed pytest runs can execute test code; Sprint 32 must keep policy/approval checks in front of tests.run.
next_sprint: FUNC-SPRINT-32 — tests.run como herramienta MIASI controlada
